reasoning: Pgm3 encodes phosphoglucomutase 3, a key enzyme that generates UDP‑GlcNAc for the hexosamine biosynthetic pathway and consequently for N‑linked glycosylation of nascent proteins. Reducing Pgm3 activity lowers cellular UDP‑GlcNAc pools, impairing proper N‑glycosylation of many secretory and membrane proteins. Defective glycosylation can cause accumulation of misfolded proteins in the endoplasmic reticulum, activating the unfolded protein response (UPR). A core component of the UPR is the transcriptional up‑regulation of ER‑resident folding catalysts, including the protein disulfide isomerase family member PDIA3 (also known as ERp57), which works with calnexin/calreticulin to assist the folding of glycoproteins. Therefore, CRISPRi knock‑down of Pgm3 is expected to trigger ER stress and lead to increased expression of Pdia3.
